qa.verdict: Already-implemented confirmation verified — no code changes needed
qa.overall_assessment: All three smoke-test checks confirmed independently by QA filesystem grep. Codex ran a full end-to-end live hook probe confirming pre_rc=0 with matching sentinel (main-agent), post_rc=0 with sentinel_exists_after=no, and no_sentinel_pre_rc=2 (correct block). The dev report is accurate: status=completed, files_modified=[], files_created=[], all three verification_results true. Two minor non-blocking findings (scope citation error, AC4 git-diff recipe drift).
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: QA grep: match_sentinel_grant_for_write at allowlist.py:523 (definition), pretool-write-guard.sh:69 (import) and :96 (call). git show HEAD:posttool-allowlist-consume.py | grep returned line 158. Codex live smoke: pre_rc=0 with sentinel, post_rc=0, sentinel_exists_after=no, no_sentinel_pre_rc=2.

dimension_1_evidence_quality.detail: BA's core evidence is verified: all three grep/Read citations are correct for HEAD e109534c. One citation inaccuracy found in the Scope section (Contract B): ticket line 54 states 'posttool-allowlist-consume.py:95 (import in top-level and line 413 in write path)'. Actual HEAD values are import at line 37 and Write sentinel call at line 181. 'Line 413' does not exist in HEAD. This appears to be a residual worktree line-number artifact. BA correctly uses HEAD citations in Evidence (Contract A) and the acceptance criteria, so this is isolated to the scope enumeration.

dimension_2_scope_alignment.detail: BA's 'already implemented' conclusion matches the verbatim requirement. The original requirement asked to integrate sentinel-grant checking into pretool-write-guard.sh for Write-overwrite operations — all three components (allowlist.py function, pretool check, posttool consume) are present. The subagent-scope gap is a legitimate out_of_scope_observation: the original prior-cycle AC2 explicitly declared is_subagent=false. No scope shrinkage or renaming detected.

dimension_3_investigation_completeness.detail: BA investigated all three target files (pretool-write-guard.sh, allowlist.py, posttool-allowlist-consume.py), the grant-writer (userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh), and performed a live Codex bash probe confirming rc=0 for main-agent with matching sentinel. The component chain is fully documented with evidence_type for each link. QA independently confirmed all findings.

dimension_4_affected_file_accuracy.detail: The ticket's Scope section (line 54) cites posttool-allowlist-consume.py:95 for the import and 'line 413 in write path' — both incorrect for HEAD e109534c. HEAD actual values: import at line 37 (from lib.allowlist import ...), match_sentinel_grant_for_write call at line 181. The AC-level citations (AC3: line 158 for the Write branch) are correct. The error is confined to the informational Scope enumeration and does not affect verifiability.

correct_head_values.import_match_sentinel: line 37 (same import block, match_sentinel_grant_for_write at line 37)

dimension_5_spec_text_vs_execution_drift.detail: AC4 as written creates a drift risk: the literal check is 'git diff HEAD -- hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh hooks/lib/allowlist.py hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py shows no changes introduced by this cycle'. However, posttool-allowlist-consume.py is ALREADY dirty at baseline (pre-existing modification noted in ticket Risk 2 and Out-of-Scope Observations). Running AC4's literal command WILL return posttool-allowlist-consume.py even if dev makes zero changes, causing a false-fail. The AC's intent is 'no cycle-introduced changes', but the literal recipe cannot distinguish pre-existing dirt from new dirt without a baseline snapshot. PROPOSED_FIX: AC4's git diff check should be scoped to only the two clean files (pretool-write-guard.sh and allowlist.py), or should compare against the baseline_dirty_snapshot to exclude pre-existing modifications.

ac_verification.description: No code modifications made by dev
ac_verification.command_run: Inspection of dev approach (strategy: NO CODE CHANGES REQUIRED) + BA's own declaration
ac_verification.output: The AC's literal git diff recipe returns posttool-allowlist-consume.py even with zero dev changes (pre-existing dirty worktree). This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift issue. The intent — that dev must not modify the hook files — is verifiable by checking only the two clean files (pretool-write-guard.sh and allowlist.py have zero diff vs HEAD). The BA has declared status=already_implemented and the development_approach explicitly states 'NO CODE CHANGES REQUIRED'.
ac_verification.note: Minor drift: literal AC4 is ambiguous against a pre-dirty worktree. Does not affect core pass verdict since BA and context JSON clearly document the pre-existing posttool dirty state.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The root cause was a duplicate orchestrator dispatch (the implementation was completed in cycle 20260522-080646-B, commit becffc6). BA correctly identified this and produced a verification-only cycle conclusion. QA independently confirmed all three implementation pieces at HEAD e109534c.

all_findings.issue: Scope section (Contract B) cites posttool-allowlist-consume.py:95 for the import and 'line 413 in write path'. Correct HEAD e109534c values: import at line 37, Write sentinel call at line 181. 'Line 413' does not exist in HEAD. These appear to be worktree line-number artifacts that leaked into the scope enumeration.
all_findings.recommendation: Correct Contract B scope section: import line 37, Write sentinel call line 181. Remove 'line 413' reference.

all_findings.issue: AC4's literal git diff recipe ('git diff HEAD -- hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh hooks/lib/allowlist.py hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py shows no changes') returns posttool-allowlist-consume.py even when dev makes zero changes, because that file is pre-dirty in the working tree. The AC intent is clear (no cycle-introduced changes) but the literal execution recipe is ambiguous.
all_findings.recommendation: Rewrite AC4's git diff check to use only the two clean files (pretool-write-guard.sh and allowlist.py), or add explicit exclusion of posttool-allowlist-consume.py from the diff check with a note that it is pre-dirty at baseline.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Subagent Write-overwrites are still blocked even when a matching sentinel exists, because the entire sentinel check is inside the _WG_IS_SUB != '1' guard. The original prior-cycle AC2 explicitly scoped is_subagent=false, so this is an expected limitation — but may require a follow-on cycle if subagents need authorized Write-overwrites.

spec_section_updates.section_4: All three sentinel-grant Write integration components verified present at HEAD e109534c: (1) match_sentinel_grant_for_write() defined at allowlist.py:523-576; (2) sentinel check wired at pretool-write-guard.sh:60-116 (import line 69, call line 96); (3) Write consume branch at posttool-allowlist-consume.py:158 (HEAD). BA conclusion 'already implemented for main-agent scope' is correct. Two minor findings: scope citation error (line 413 does not exist in HEAD; correct call is line 181) and AC4 git-diff recipe ambiguity against pre-dirty worktree.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 6 issues: (1) AC2 evidence needed live hook smoke rather than symbol grep — Codex itself ran the smoke (pre_rc=0/2, sentinel_exists_after=no). (2) AC3 needed behavioral evidence beyond grep — posttool smoke confirmed grant consumed. (3) Line citation drift: report should cite HEAD line 158, not worktree line 222. (4) 'No new files' overclaims workflow artifacts — scoped to implementation files. (5) AC4 literal git diff ambiguous against pre-dirty worktree — noted as minor non-blocking finding. (6) Subagent scope gap — documented as out_of_scope_observation. All PROPOSED_FIX items addressed in report.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Live smoke test evidence added for AC2/AC3 (pre_rc=0, post_rc=0, sentinel_exists_after=no). HEAD line citations used exclusively (line 158, not worktree line 222). AC4 drift and scope citation inaccuracy recorded as minor findings. Subagent limitation documented as out_of_scope_observation. Core verdict unchanged: PASS.
